intro:
Body Mass Index (BMI) is a widely used screening tool that estimates body fat based on your height and weight. It provides a quick, cost-free way to assess whether you fall into a healthy weight range for your height. While BMI does not directly measure body fat percentage, research has shown it correlates well with more direct measures of body fat for most people.

BMI is calculated by dividing your weight in kilograms by the square of your height in meters (kg/m²). The resulting number places you in one of four categories: Underweight (below 18.5), Normal weight (18.5–24.9), Overweight (25–29.9), or Obese (30 and above). These thresholds, established by the World Health Organization, apply to most adults aged 18–65 regardless of gender or ethnicity.

Our BMI calculator instantly computes your BMI and shows you the healthy weight range for your height, helping you make informed decisions about your health and wellness goals.
howItWorks: Enter your weight in kilograms and your height in centimeters. The calculator divides your weight by the square of your height (in meters) to produce your BMI score. It then classifies your result into the standard WHO categories and shows you the healthy weight range (BMI 18.5–24.9) for your specific height.
interpretationGuide: A BMI under 18.5 suggests you may be underweight, which can signal nutritional deficiencies. A BMI of 18.5–24.9 is considered healthy for most adults. A BMI of 25–29.9 indicates overweight, associated with increased health risks. A BMI of 30 or above is classified as obese, which significantly raises the risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and other conditions. BMI is a screening tool, not a diagnostic measure — always consult a healthcare professional for a full assessment.

faqs.question: What is a healthy BMI for adults?
faqs.answer: For most adults, a BMI between 18.5 and 24.9 is considered healthy. Values below 18.5 are underweight, 25–29.9 is overweight, and 30 or above is obese.

faqs.question: Is BMI accurate for athletes and bodybuilders?
faqs.answer: BMI can overestimate body fat in athletes and muscular individuals because muscle weighs more than fat. An athlete may have a high BMI while having low body fat. In these cases, body fat percentage is a better measure.

faqs.question: Does BMI differ for men and women?
faqs.answer: The standard BMI formula and categories are the same for men and women. However, women naturally carry more body fat than men at the same BMI, so some researchers argue for gender-specific thresholds.

faqs.question: Is BMI valid for children?
faqs.answer: Standard adult BMI categories do not apply to children and teenagers. For those under 18, BMI-for-age percentile charts are used instead, which account for normal changes in body composition as children grow.

faqs.question: What are the limitations of BMI?
faqs.answer: BMI does not distinguish between fat mass and lean mass, does not account for fat distribution (visceral vs. subcutaneous), and may be less accurate for certain ethnic groups, older adults, and pregnant women.

faqs.question: Can BMI predict health risks?
faqs.answer: At the population level, higher BMI correlates with increased risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, hypertension, sleep apnea, and certain cancers. However, it is a screening tool, not a direct health diagnostic.

faqs.question: How is BMI different from body fat percentage?
faqs.answer: BMI is an indirect estimate based on height and weight. Body fat percentage directly measures the proportion of fat to total body weight using methods like DEXA scans, hydrostatic weighing, or skinfold calipers.

faqs.question: What should I do if my BMI is in the overweight range?
faqs.answer: A BMI in the overweight range is a prompt to review your diet and physical activity. Even modest weight loss of 5–10% can significantly reduce health risks. Consult a doctor or registered dietitian for personalized guidance.

faqs.question: How do I convert weight and height for the calculator?
faqs.answer: To convert pounds to kilograms, divide by 2.205 (e.g., 160 lbs ÷ 2.205 ≈ 72.6 kg). To convert feet and inches to centimeters, multiply feet by 30.48 and inches by 2.54, then add them together.

faqs.question: What is the difference between overweight and obese?
faqs.answer: Overweight (BMI 25–29.9) means carrying more weight than is considered healthy but not yet at a level that severely impairs health. Obese (BMI ≥ 30) significantly increases the risk of serious medical conditions and is further classified into Class I (30–34.9), Class II (35–39.9), and Class III (≥ 40).
